title: Foreign aid given for emergencies and longer-term development
subtitle: Committed bilateral [official development assistance (ODA)](#dod:oda) from governments and multilateral organizations, grants from civil society organizations. Aid for emergencies, such as natural disasters and wars, is often called humanitarian aid. This data is expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.
caption:
Foreign aid given for emergencies and longer-term development

Committed bilateral official development assistance (ODA) from governments and multilateral organizations, grants from civil society organizations. Aid for emergencies, such as natural disasters and wars, is often called humanitarian aid. This data is expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.

The chart displays foreign aid trends from 1971 to 2024, measured in constant 2023 US dollars, with values stacked to show the total volume. The lower pink band represents aid for emergencies, while the upper dark green area represents aid for longer-term development. From 1971 through the late 1990s, the pink band remains very thin, generally staying below the $10 billion mark, with a slight rise visible around 1990. The green band dominates the total height during this early period, fluctuating between roughly $40 billion and $70 billion, with a notable spike reaching nearly $100 billion around 1991 before receding. Starting in the early 2000s, both categories show an upward trajectory. The pink band expands more visibly after 2000, rising from near zero to occupy a band between $10 billion and $20 billion by 2010, and reaching approximately $30 billion by 2024. The green band also grows significantly during this time, climbing from roughly $60 billion in 2000 to over $140 billion by 2020. The total height of the chart peaks sharply around 2023, exceeding $180 billion, before dropping slightly in the final data point for 2024 to a level near $170 billion. Throughout the entire period, the green area consistently comprises the majority of the total aid volume.
